turns.note: All three definitions match slide 2 of the intro deck (LLM language-only; VLM = LLM + vision encoder, generates text; MLLM = text/images/video/audio); source_mode file.

Says no per the deck (VLM understands language+images, generates text only) and contrasts with MLLM; the added hedge 'not all MLLMs do' is a mild qualification beyond the deck but invents no VLM capability.
Three-row table (LLM/VLM/MLLM) with input/output modality columns; VLM output = text only, MLLM covers all four modalities; no invented fourth row.

Matching exercise left pairs perfectly aligned (1-A, 2-B, 3-C, unshuffled) AND printed 'Answer Key: 1-A, 2-B, 3-C' inside the exercise itself, violating both the shuffle and the no-answers-in-exercise expectations.
Key 1-A/2-B/3-C is correct and consistent with the Turn 4 exercise, though it was already redundant since Turn 4 leaked it.
Names 01_dvlm_introduction.pptx and correctly pinpoints Slide 2 (verified: the definitions are on slide 2); no other file claimed.

highlight: Definitions, table, and source naming are verbatim-correct, but the student-facing matching exercise shipped with unshuffled pairs and its answer key printed inline — exactly what the teacher would need to strip out by hand.
